ranked_movies.title: The Lobster
ranked_movies.reason: The central film of the most upvoted post, described as hilarious, horrifying, and deeply weird, sparking extensive discussion and numerous recommendations for similar films.

ranked_movies.title: The Act of Killing
ranked_movies.reason: Highly recommended and discussed in multiple threads for its mind-blowing and unsettling portrayal of mass murderers, appearing in both 'traumatizing' and 'documentary' requests.

ranked_movies.title: Three Identical Strangers
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently cited as a shocking and mind-blowing documentary across multiple high-scoring posts requesting impactful non-fiction films.

ranked_movies.title: Irreversible
ranked_movies.reason: Extensively discussed for its traumatic and technically disturbing nature in the thread seeking films that will 'severely fuck you up'.

ranked_movies.title: The Killing of a Sacred Deer
ranked_movies.reason: The most recommended follow-up to 'The Lobster' from the same director, noted for its disturbing and brilliant themes.

ranked_movies.title: Happiness
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended multiple times as a chilling 'comedy' and a film with deeply disturbing themes that fits the request for traumatic viewing.

ranked_movies.title: Logan
ranked_movies.reason: The subject of a highly-upvoted post praising it as an unexpected masterpiece in the superhero genre, cited for its brutal action and compelling story.

ranked_movies.title: Kids
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently mentioned as a core, traumatic memory-inducing film with a powerful and disturbing ending.

ranked_movies.title: Mysterious Skin
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended multiple times as a film that 'shook me to my core' and is extremely dark.

ranked_movies.title: The Jinx
ranked_movies.reason: A top recommendation in the documentary requests for being intense, eye-opening, and unsettling.

ranked_movies.title: Requiem for a Dream
ranked_movies.reason: The catalyst for the entire 'traumatize me' thread, establishing the benchmark for a genuinely good but horrifying film.

ranked_movies.title: Dogtooth
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed as a favorite film by Yorgos Lanthimos, the director of 'The Lobster', noted for being deeply memorable and thought-provoking.

ranked_movies.title: The Emperor's New Groove
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted as a surprisingly excellent movie that no one expected to be good, earning its love as one of Disney's best.

ranked_movies.title: EuroTrip
ranked_movies.reason: The subject of its own highly-upvoted post, celebrated as an excellent dumb comedy with a cult following and memorable moments.

ranked_movies.title: Boogie Nights
ranked_movies.reason: Cited in the discussion about artistic pornographic films as a prime example depicting the 70s porn industry.

ranked_movies.title: The Shining
ranked_movies.reason: The subject of a deep analytical post on r/TrueFilm, sparking discussion about its characters and themes.

ranked_movies.title: The Florida Project
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a powerful depiction of poverty and addiction, and later cited as an accurate portrayal of grimy, difficult living conditions.

ranked_movies.title: Gladiator
ranked_movies.reason: The original film was the benchmark against which its sequel was critically compared in a discussion about Ridley Scott's recent work.

ranked_movies.title: Galaxy Quest
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as a movie with very low expectations that blew viewers away with its quality and humor.

ranked_movies.title: Caligula
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended in the thread about artistic pornographic films as a notable example.
